summary: Russian operators of the "Rubikon" Center destroyed 132 Ukrainian fixed-wing UAVs on March 13, 2026, with 111 targets hit the previous day, including personnel and UAV control points in Donbas, Sumy, and Krasny Liman. The "Yolka" anti-drone system conducted multiple kinetic intercepts of Ukrainian fixed-wing drones in spring 2026. Ukrainian forces increased FPV drone deliveries by at least 50% year-over-year, with February deliveries up 17% from January, while deploying tactics like using FPVs to dismount Russian troops and targeting Russian infantry with simultaneous strikes. Ukraine also received two Phantom MK-1 humanoid robots for testing, capable of resupplying troops, scouting, and using human weapons.
